segments.text: First, listen, then answer the following question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: Why is there no risk to the customer when a bank prints the customer's name on his checks?

translations.zh-Hans: 银行把客户姓名印在支票上，为什么不会给客户带来风险？

segments.text: When anyone opens a current account at a bank, he is lending the bank money, repayment of which he may demand at any time, either in cash or by drawing a check in favor of another person.

translations.zh-Hans: 任何人在银行开立活期账户，都是在把钱借给银行；他可以随时要求银行偿还，方式可以是提取现金，也可以是开支票给他人。

segments.text: Primarily, the banker-customer relationship His is that of debtor and creditor.

translations.zh-Hans: 从根本上说，银行与客户的关系是债务人与债权人的关系。

segments.text: Who is which, depending on whether the customer's account is in credit or is overdrawn?

translations.zh-Hans: 谁是债务人、谁是债权人，要看客户账户有余额还是已经透支。

segments.text: But in addition to that basically simple concept, the bank and its customer owe a large number of obligations to one another.

translations.zh-Hans: 但除了这个基本而简单的概念外，银行和客户彼此还承担许多义务。

segments.text: Many of these obligations can give rise to problems and complications, but a bank customer, unlike, say, a buyer of goods, cannot complain that the law is loaded against him.

translations.zh-Hans: 其中许多义务会引发问题和复杂情况；然而银行客户不同于商品买家等人，不能抱怨法律偏袒对方。

segments.text: The bank must obey its customer's instructions and not those of anyone else.

translations.zh-Hans: 银行必须服从客户的指示，而不能听从任何其他人的指示。

segments.text: When, for example, a customer first opens an account, he instructs the bank to debit his account only in respect of checks drawn by himself.

translations.zh-Hans: 例如，客户首次开户时，会指示银行只有在支票由本人签发时才能从其账户扣款。

segments.text: He gives the bank specimens of his signature, and there is a very firm rule that the bank has no right or authority to pay out a customer's money on a check on which its customer's signature has been forged.

translations.zh-Hans: 他向银行提供签名样本；一项非常严格的规则是，如果支票上的客户签名是伪造的，银行无权从客户账户付款。

segments.text: It makes no difference that the forgery may have been a very skillful one.

translations.zh-Hans: 即使伪造得极其高明，也没有区别。

segments.text: bank must recognize its customer's signature.

translations.zh-Hans: 银行必须能够识别客户的签名。

segments.text: For this reason, there is no risk to the customer in the practice adopted by banks of printing the customer's name on his checks.

translations.zh-Hans: 正因如此，银行在支票上印上客户姓名的做法并不会给客户带来风险。

segments.text: If this facilitates forgery, it is the bank which will lose, not the customer.

translations.zh-Hans: 如果这样做方便了伪造，蒙受损失的是银行，而不是客户。
